description_long: This is a sleek, modern jet airplane with a predominantly black fuselage and sharply swept wings, giving it a stealthy, aggressive appearance. The wings are low-mounted and feature slight anhedral angles, with small wingtip extensions that may function as winglets. The rear of the airplane is equipped with two prominent jet engines, each with orange and gray exhaust nozzles, and a central tailpipe, suggesting a high-performance propulsion system. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers set at an outward angle, forming a distinctive V-shape, and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a light blue-tinted canopy that appears to be enclosed and streamlined. The landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable and integrated into the fuselage or wings.
description: A black, swept-wing jet airplane with twin vertical stabilizers, dual rear jet engines, and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Rear view showing dual jet engines with orange exhausts, twin outward-angled vertical stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the long, slender fuselage, swept-back wings, and the prominent engine nacelles at the rear.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and the wide, low-mounted wings with wingtip extensions.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile mirroring view 1, showing the same engine and wing configuration from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept wings with slight anhedral angle and small wingtip extensions.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: V-tail configuration with twin outward-angled vertical stabilizers and a connecting horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy and light blue-tinted windows.
